svg.text: Bitmask N-Queens — Place One Queen per Row
Three masks track forbidden columns at the current row
bit = available & −available # lowest set bit (= candidate column)
Each row's diag1 shifts left by 1; diag2 shifts right by 1.
No undo needed — masks are passed by value, restored automatically.
• Symmetry: solutions come in groups of 8 (4 rotations × 2 reflections), or 4 if symmetric.
Solve only the first half of column 0 in row 0, then double-count (with care for symmetric ones).
• Bitmask: low-bit tricks (x & −x) iterate set bits in O(popcount) instead of O(n).
• Branch ordering: most-constrained row first ≈ MRV (rare for n-queens; rows are interchangeable).
• Distributed: Knuth's "Estimating the efficiency of backtrack programs" gives sampling-based
predictions of search-tree size — useful for sharding the work across machines.
